recipes.name: Miso-Glazed Salmon with Edamame Rice
recipes.nameEs: Salmón Glaseado con Miso y Arroz de Edamame
recipes.description: Caramelised miso-glazed salmon seared in a pan, served over sushi rice loaded with edamame. Japanese flavours, simple execution, serious protein.

steps.instruction: Mix miso paste, soy sauce, sesame oil, and minced garlic into a glaze. Coat salmon fillets and marinate 10 min.
steps.tip: Miso burns fast — keep the heat at medium-high, not high.

steps.instruction: Cook rice per packet instructions. Stir in edamame in the last 3 min of cooking.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium-high. Sear salmon 3–4 min per side until caramelised and just cooked through.
steps.tip: Don't move it — let it caramelise undisturbed for a proper crust.

steps.instruction: Serve salmon over edamame rice.

storageNotes.reheatingMethod: Rice in microwave 2 min with a splash of water. Salmon in a pan over medium-low 3 min.
storageNotes.portionNotes: Store salmon and rice separately to keep textures intact.

proteinBuffer.name: Hard-boiled eggs + canned tuna
proteinBuffer.description: 3 hard-boiled eggs + 100g canned tuna with a squeeze of lemon

recipes.name: Garlic Shrimp & White Bean Stew
recipes.nameEs: Estofado de Gambas con Alubias Blancas y Ajo
recipes.description: A Mediterranean one-pot with garlicky shrimp, creamy white beans, crushed tomatoes, and wilted spinach. Ready in 20 minutes, loaded with protein.

steps.instruction: Heat olive oil in a wide pan over medium-high. Add minced garlic and cook 1 min until fragrant.
Add smoked paprika and oregano. Stir 30 seconds to toast the spices.
steps.tip: Toasting spices in the oil unlocks their full aroma — don't skip this.

steps.instruction: Add crushed tomatoes and white beans. Simmer 8 min.
Add shrimp, cook 3–4 min until pink. Stir in spinach until wilted.
steps.tip: Shrimp overcook fast — pull them off the heat the moment they curl and turn pink.

steps.instruction: Season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately.

storageNotes.reheatingMethod: Stovetop over medium-low heat with a splash of water, 4 min. Do not microwave shrimp.
storageNotes.portionNotes: Shrimp texture degrades after 2 days — eat the second portion by next day if possible.

proteinBuffer.name: Smoked salmon roll-ups
proteinBuffer.description: 100g smoked salmon rolled with cucumber slices and a squeeze of lemon

recipes.name: Air Fryer Soy-Garlic Cod with Broccoli
recipes.nameEs: Bacalao con Soja y Ajo en Air Fryer con Brócoli
recipes.description: Flaky cod marinated in soy, garlic and sesame — air-fried alongside crispy broccoli and served over rice. Clean, fast, Japanese-inspired.

steps.instruction: Mix soy sauce, minced garlic, and sesame oil. Coat cod and marinate 10 min.
Toss broccoli florets with a drizzle of olive oil and a pinch of salt.
Air fry broccoli at 200°C for 8 min, shaking at 4 min.
steps.tip: Remove broccoli and set aside before loading the cod.

steps.instruction: Air fry cod at 190°C for 10 min, flipping at 5 min. Check at 9 min — do not overcook.
steps.tip: Cod is done when it flakes easily and is opaque through. Pull it early if in doubt.

steps.instruction: Serve cod over rice with broccoli alongside. Drizzle any remaining marinade over the top.

airFryerSettings.notes: Broccoli first: 200°C / 8 min / shake at 4 min. Cod after: 190°C / 10 min / flip at 5 min.

storageNotes.reheatingMethod: Air fryer at 175°C for 5 min. Rice in microwave with a splash of water.
storageNotes.portionNotes: Keep broccoli, cod, and rice in separate containers to reheat independently.

proteinBuffer.name: Lactose-free soy yogurt + protein powder
proteinBuffer.description: 150g lactose-free soy yogurt blended with 1 scoop unflavoured protein powder

recipes.name: Tuna Niçoise Bowl
recipes.description: Fresh tuna steak seared rare-to-medium over blanched green beans, cherry tomatoes, jammy eggs, and olives — dressed with a caper vinaigrette. High protein, low carb.

steps.instruction: Boil eggs for 7 min for a jammy yolk. Transfer to ice water, peel, and halve.
steps.tip: Ice bath stops the cooking immediately — don't skip it or you'll overshoot.

steps.instruction: Blanch green beans in boiling salted water for 3 min. Drain and cool under cold water.
Season tuna with salt and pepper. Sear in a very hot pan with olive oil: 90 sec per side for rare-medium. Rest 2 min, then slice.
steps.tip: The pan must be smoking hot. Press down gently for even contact.

steps.instruction: Make dressing: whisk 2 tbsp olive oil + red wine vinegar + finely chopped capers + salt + pepper.
Arrange green beans, tomatoes, and olives in bowls. Top with sliced tuna and halved eggs. Drizzle dressing over everything.

storageNotes.reheatingMethod: Eat cold or at room temperature — this bowl is better not reheated. Dress just before eating.
storageNotes.portionNotes: Keep dressing separate. Tuna slices well cold the next day.

proteinBuffer.name: Canned tuna with crackers
proteinBuffer.description: 100g canned tuna in olive oil with rice crackers and a squeeze of lemon

recipes.name: Prawn & Chickpea Masala
recipes.description: A bold, warming Indian-inspired one-pot with juicy prawns, hearty chickpeas, and a garam masala tomato base. High protein, meal-prep friendly.

steps.instruction: Sauté finely diced onion in olive oil over medium heat for 5 min until soft. Add minced garlic and cook 1 more min.
Add garam masala and turmeric. Stir 30 seconds to toast the spices in the oil.
steps.tip: Toasting spices is non-negotiable — it's the difference between a flat and a deep flavour.

steps.instruction: Add crushed tomatoes and chickpeas. Simmer 10 min.
Add prawns and cook 3–4 min until pink and curled. Do not overcook.
steps.tip: Pull off heat as soon as every prawn is pink — they keep cooking from residual heat.

steps.instruction: Serve over basmati rice.

storageNotes.reheatingMethod: Stovetop over medium-low with a splash of water, 4–5 min. Rice separately in microwave.
storageNotes.portionNotes: Prawns tighten up on day 2 but the masala base actually improves overnight.

proteinBuffer.name: Canned tuna + white bean mash on rice cakes
proteinBuffer.description: 100g canned tuna + mashed white beans + lemon on 3 rice cakes

recipes.name: Air Fryer Salmon Tacos
recipes.nameEs: Tacos de Salmón en Air Fryer
recipes.description: Spiced air-fried salmon broken into chunks, loaded into warm corn tortillas with crunchy lime slaw and a smoky mayo drizzle. Bold, fresh, and fast.

steps.instruction: Season salmon with smoked paprika, cumin, salt, and a drizzle of olive oil.
Air fry salmon at 200°C for 12 min, flipping at 6 min.
steps.tip: Salmon should be just cooked through and lightly charred at the edges.

steps.instruction: While salmon cooks, toss shredded red cabbage with lime juice, a drizzle of olive oil, and a pinch of salt.
Mix mayo with a pinch of smoked paprika for the drizzle sauce.
Warm tortillas in the air fryer at 180°C for 1 min.
Flake salmon into chunks. Fill each tortilla with salmon, lime slaw, and a drizzle of paprika mayo.

airFryerSettings.notes: Salmon: 200°C / 12 min / flip at 6 min. Tortillas: 180°C / 1 min to warm through.

storageNotes.reheatingMethod: Reheat salmon in air fryer at 180°C for 4 min. Warm tortillas fresh — they go soggy stored assembled.
storageNotes.portionNotes: Store components separately: salmon, slaw, and tortillas all in their own containers.

proteinBuffer.name: Egg white omelette with smoked salmon
proteinBuffer.description: 4 egg whites + 80g smoked salmon, cooked as a thin omelette with a squeeze of lemon
